cold_open.hook: 30-year Treasury yield just hit 5.18%—highest since before 2008. The bond market is choosing violence today.

markets.macro_note: Equities red across the board; rates spiking; pharma rallying while semiconductors and mega-cap tech crater.

big_story.story_title: In Musk v. Altman trial, the entire AI industry lost
big_story.angle: Two billionaires fighting over OpenAI's soul while the actual story—that neither side can explain what they're building or why it's worth $600 billion—goes completely unexamined in court.

quick_hits.angle: CISA, the agency literally tasked with protecting critical infrastructure, left passwords and cloud keys on the open web—this is the cybersecurity equivalent of a bank leaving the vault combo on a Post-it.
Congress scrambling to regulate prediction markets right as they're becoming the only honest price discovery mechanism left in finance—regulators always show up to the party three years late.
MAHA-era FDA is loosening rules on vapes and tanning beds because apparently we're speedrunning the 1980s health crisis playbook.
Oil hits highest price since Iran war started; Deutsche Bank says markets are sleeping on the actual shock risk—this stock has commitment issues with reality.

odd_thing.joke_angle: Xbox fans demanding free online multiplayer and exclusives is like asking your landlord to lower rent while also renovating the apartment—pick a lane, gamers.

yesterday_callback.topic: KeyBanc bumped CrowdStrike to $700 on 'Mythos' hype—this is the Crocs IPO of cybersecurity
yesterday_callback.fresh_take: Today CISA literally exposed passwords to the open web, which means the entire cybersecurity hype cycle is built on theater—CrowdStrike's $700 price target just became the Crocs IPO of corporate governance.

sign_off.callback_target: CISA leaving passwords on the open web
